The Vault v0.5.0 adds LLM-Hub as a second AI provider, closes the enrichment loop, and hardens the task executor.

Highlights:
- LLM-Hub provider support: any OpenAI-compatible hub can now power the task executor, enrichment, and the Vault API backend. Configure a custom base URL and API key in Settings; the model dropdowns load live from the hub's /models endpoint. OpenRouter remains the default with its fixed endpoint.
- Closed enrichment loop: completed enrich/organize duty tasks now return structured JSON that Vault validates and applies back to the source memory — tags and keywords merge additively, summaries upgrade only when genuinely more informative, and destructive actions (merges, deletions) stay human-decided proposals. Every apply is auditable on the task result and activity log.
- Task executor recovery: tasks orphaned in running status by an interrupted executor are automatically requeued or failed, so queue stats no longer show phantom running tasks after a crash or app quit.
- Terminal-state integrity: completed, failed, and cancelled are now final task states; cancelling a task mid-execution wins over the in-flight model call, and cancelled tasks can no longer be resurrected by retries.
- Recall-quality task memories: delegated task results are persisted with a real topical subject, clean summaries (executor annotations stripped), stopword-filtered keywords, and pass-through tags — so they rank properly in recall instead of polluting it.
- Better project description proposals: the agent review duty now drafts descriptions only with AI evidence from high-signal memories (promoted items, decisions, plans, references), eliminating the item-count boilerplate suggestions.
- Reorganized Settings: the runtime mega-tab is now three focused tabs — Overview, AI & Models, and Memory & Recall.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
